items.briefing:
Phishing attacks are becoming more sophisticated and harder to spot, with AI now being used to craft convincing fake emails at scale — a direct concern for staff at training organisations who regularly handle enrolments, payments, and personal student data. Separately, a Chinese cybercrime group known as TA4922 is expanding its attacks globally, which may increasingly affect Australian businesses and institutions.

On a positive note, Google is rolling out fake call detection on Android phones in Australia, which can help protect staff from voice-based scams. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email or call asking you to click a link or share login details, verify the request through a separate, known contact method before acting.

featured.title: Google rolls out fake call detection on Android phones
featured.quote: Android's new spoofed call detection directly protects millions of Australian smartphone users from identity fraud.
